metadata.title:
CHAMBERLAIN


URGES UNITY

INVITES GOVERNORS OF ALL
STATES TO UNITE FOR A
POLICY OF SAFEGUARDING
NATURAL RESOURCES FOR
FUTURE GENERATIONS.

pages.raw_text:
WASHINGTON, Dec. 8.--A new
policy giving the federal govern
ment absolute control over
streams, forests and mineral re-
serves by the creation of an inter-
state conservation commission was
advocated by Governor Chamber
lain today in his address before
the joint conservation congress.


He recommended abolishing
state lines in the administration of
national resources. He called upon
the governors of every state to
join his policy and was promised
cooepration on part of the west.


I'VE will probably agree upon
one point" he said, that the con-
servation of our natural resources
is necessary to the well being of
the country and the protection of
generations yet unborn. Upon the
perpetuation of our institutions, as
to the means to be adopted. we
may differ"


He reviewed the creation of the
national conservation commission
by President Roosevelt, and de
clared that the question as to what
policy ought to be adopted for the
future was the paramount issue
before the present congress. He
said:


"One of two policies must be
adotped in order to succeed That
II must be either a national or q
state policy: that can empower
congress to be supreme regarding
the soil, mines, forests. streams
tributary to navigable waterways
there is no question"


He then quoted decisions of the
United States supreme court to
show the extent of jurisdiction of
national government over navi-
gable waterways. he continued:


IF this power and jurisdiction
be recognized, may it not be in.
sisted that it is within the powers
of congress to enact uniform laws,
not only to safeguard water tribu-
taries to navigahle waterways. but
to provide for the distribution of
such waters for the beneficial use
in the reclamation of the arid and
semi-arid lands of the country"
